meaning: adj.刺耳的,不和谐的,辗轧的n.震动,冲突,辗轧声
examples: In the context of this chapter, Dore's comments strike a jarring note.
The RA of two years added that while some conflicts "provided more multicultural acceptance and melding", there were also "jarring cultural confrontations".
There was a jarring note of triumph in his voice.
Our cycle of job creation and destruction can be jarring, and perhaps even unfair at times.
Indeed, the monarchist army chief, General Rookmangud Katwal, this week struck a jarring note.
